title: Implement general Galois field GF(p^m) arithmetic
description:
Implement prime field arithmetic for GF(p) and general extension fields GF(p^m). This is the foundational algebraic expansion from GF(2)/GF(2^m) to a general-purpose finite field toolkit. See linked design documents for detailed architecture. ## Success Criteria - **Trait hierarchy**: `FiniteField` + `ConstField` + `FiniteFieldExt` implemented and proven across `Gf2mElement`, `Fp

`, `Gf2_8`/`Gf2_16`, and `QuadraticExt` - **Wide accumulator**: Associated `Wide` type on `FiniteField` for bounds-aware delayed reduction, enabling generic matrix kernels to accumulate without field-specific code (inspired by fflas-ffpack's `MMHelper` pattern) - **FieldVec**: Generic `FieldVec` with scalar and SIMD-accelerated operations, including delayed-reduction dot product with bounds-aware scheduling - **Axiom tests**: All trait implementations pass property-based field axiom test harness (associativity, commutativity, distributivity, characteristic, inverse, hash consistency) - **Linear algebra readiness**: Trait expressiveness and kernel performance proven sufficient to support generic `FieldMatrix` linear algebra — the downstream linear algebra epic can build directly on this foundation without trait changes - **Performance**: Zero-overhead abstraction for const-generic types; competitive with fflas-ffpack element-level throughput for prime field arithmetic ## References - [fflas-ffpack analysis](dev/plans/fflas_ffpack_analysis.md) — design patterns and benchmark targets - [GF(p^n) groundwork analysis](dev/plans/gfpn_groundwork_analysis.md) — trait hierarchy and build order - [GF(p^n) planning session](dev/plans/gfpn_planning_session.md) — architecture exploration
